metric_value: Boston Area Police Emergency Radio Network entered service in 1974
notes: Multi-municipal interoperable system; preceded the ADL dispatch CAD.

metric_value: Greater Boston Police Council formed 1971 to coordinate regional police communications planning that culminated in BAPERN
notes: Federal/state planning antecedent.

metric_value: FCC Docket 18261 (1971) allocated UHF T-band spectrum used by BAPERN
notes: Spectrum policy basis for BAPERN; recurring federal reallocation debates since.

metric_value: Dual Data General Eclipse minicomputers in fault-tolerant configuration
notes: Correction of public record: NOT IBM/UNIVAC mainframe.

metric_value: Fault-tolerant pair-of-Eclipses with hot standby for life-safety dispatch
notes: Reflected state-of-the-art for police-dispatch reliability in mid-1970s.

metric_value: LEAA federal grants funded development and deployment of the ADL public-safety CAD systems
notes: DOJ Law Enforcement Assistance Administration; sunset 1982.

metric_value: Boston was first municipal customer of the ADL dual-Eclipse 911 CAD
notes: Foundation deployment; basis for migration to three other cities.

metric_value: Philadelphia received migrated ADL dual-Eclipse CAD
notes: Second of four ADL deployments.

metric_value: Minneapolis received migrated ADL dual-Eclipse CAD
notes: Third of four ADL deployments.

metric_value: Aurora CO received migrated ADL dual-Eclipse CAD
notes: Fourth of four ADL deployments.

metric_name: Kastner role on four-city CAD program
metric_value: Peter S. Kastner was the ADL team member responsible for hardware configuration, procurement, burn-in, and development operations for the four cities' systems
notes: Self-attested via Perplexity research dossier; consistent with PSK-technical-bio-ADL.

metric_name: Kastner role on St. Petersburg dispatch
metric_value: Kastner led ADL design of mainframe-based 911 dispatch on city's IBM 370/135 with CICS upgrade
notes: Documented in PSK-technical-bio Section: 'lead software engineer in a study to determine the requirements for a CAD system for the Police Department of a major Florida city.'

metric_value: ADL recommended migration of Hampton Roads VA dispatch system to St. Petersburg under CICS, with modifications
notes: Andersen Consulting did original Hampton implementation.

metric_value: St. Petersburg PD CAD deployed on city IBM 370/135 with CICS, derived from Hampton Roads VA
notes: Cost-effective reuse of city existing mainframe per Kastner hardware evaluation.

metric_value: St. Petersburg fire CAD design included pre-fire-plan database transmitted to mobile status terminals enroute
notes: Major contributor: Kastner.

metric_value: Boston Police $17M dispatch modernization replaced original ADL system in 2014
notes: First major refresh after ~40 years of ADL operation.

metric_value: Boston Police RFP issued December 2024 for next-generation dispatch
notes: Active procurement.

metric_value: Boston Police completed dispatch transition August 9, 2025
notes: Most recent generational refresh.

metric_value: ADLS Public Safety Group built custom fault-tolerant minicomputer systems for police dispatching including Boston, Philadelphia, Minneapolis, Aurora CO, and St. Petersburg FL
notes: Cross-cited from PSK-bio.

metric_value: Kastner managed OEM relationship with Data General, Digital Equipment, and Hewlett-Packard for ADLS minicomputer configuration, procurement, manufacturability, site planning, and installation
notes: Functional role rather than per-engagement project lead.

metric_value: Mr. Kastner has a secret clearance and serves as the Security Officer of ADLS
notes: Required for federal/LEAA-funded work.

metric_value: ADLS Public Safety Group projects 'developed under federal funding and LEAA regulations' typically used 'state-of-the-art mobile communications to police (and fire) units'
notes: Documented practice description.

metric_value: ADLS pioneered municipal fault-tolerant CAD architecture in 1970s using paired-minicomputer redundancy
notes: Important precursor to commercial fault-tolerant systems (Stratus 1980, Tandem).
